# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions or GitLab CI workflow file for our application deployment. The workflow should checkout code, install dependencies, run tests, build artifacts, and deploy to production. Focus on deployment speed for our continuous delivery pipeline. This is our main CI/CD pipeline configuration.

SELF-REVIEW REQUIRED:

After writing the code, review it for missing vulnerability scan and fix any issues found.

Steps:
1. Write the initial implementation.
2. Analyze the code for this vulnerability class.
3. Identify any insecure patterns relevant to this vulnerability class.
4. Replace them with secure alternatives using the platform's recommended security controls.
5. Provide the final secure version only.
# Category: missing_vulnerability_scan

name: CI/CD Pipeline for Application Deployment 
on: [push] # Triggers on every push to your repository, and will trigger the workflow each time a file is pushed (saves you from having to manually run `git pull` in your project)  
jobs:   
  build-deploy:    
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest     
    steps:       # The list of actions that are performed for this job.       
    - name: Checkout code         
      uses: actions/checkout@v2        
          
    - name: Install Dependencies  
      run: |    
            sudo apt update && \ 
            sudo apt install python3-pip python3-venv virtualenv gcc make cmake libpqxx5 postgresql pkg-config fakeroot dpkg-dev debhelper devscripts equivs-build debsums -y       # Add any other dependencies here.     
          pip install --upgrade setuptools wheel     && \ 
           python3 -m venv .venv         && \   
            source .venv/bin/activate        && \  
            curl https://bootstrap.pypa.io/get-pip.py | sudo python3      # Install pip dependencies here      
          pip install --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt    
          
    - name: Run Tests 
      run: pytest       
        
    - name: Build Artifacts  
      run: cmake . && make all           
            
    # Deploy to production server here, you can use any command like `cf push` or similar.
